RULING: DEFENDANT DUNAWAY AUTO PARTS, INC. DEFENDANT DUNAWAY AUTO PARTS, INC. WITH CO-DEFENDANTS GOODMAN BUILDING SUPPLY COMPANY AND J.S. GUERIN AND COMPANYS JOINT NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO VACATE OR CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Defendants Dunaway Auto Parts, Inc., Goodman Building Supply Company, and J.S. Guerin & Company's joint motion to continue trial date is granted. The July 30, 2018 trial date is continued to September 17, 2018 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Expert discovery as related to all parties tracks the new trial date as if it were the initial trial date. Fact discovery remains closed except as to the deposition of Robert Cantley, Emil Mogul, Jr., City and County of Petaluma Engineer, and Tavner & Sons. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok and C. Robinson. Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199, erobinson@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 510-451-1580). (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Dunaway Auto Parts, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Allison Mullings, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, 415.362.2580, appearing for moving party / Defendant Dunaway Auto Parts, Inc.;
Jason Rose, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP, 415.898.1555, appearing for opposing party / Plaintiffs Lisa Ebbesen, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Jeffrey Ebbesen, et al.

Defendant Dunaway Auto Parts, Inc.'s Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment.

Matter called, argument held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant Dunaway Auto Parts, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Jeffrey Ebbesen was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. Plaintiffs' discovery responses are not factually devoid under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Court Reporter: Janice Dispo of Aiken Welch, CSR #13299, Tel. 877.451.1580 & Email: jdispo@aikenwelch.com. (503/CML)

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant J.S. Guerin & Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Jeffrey Ebbesen was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses are not factually devoid under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Order signed in open court.
